items.fix: Pass raw logits to nn.CrossEntropyLoss or use log_softmax + NLLLoss. The loss expects unnormalized scores.
items.why: CrossEntropyLoss already applies log-softmax; double normalization gives wrong gradients and incorrect loss values.
items.example: Wrong: probs = F.softmax(logits); loss = -torch.log(probs[y]). Right: loss = F.cross_entropy(logits, y).

items.fix: Subtract max(z) before exp, or use framework log_softmax / CrossEntropyLoss.
items.why: Large logits cause exp overflow to inf and NaN probabilities in float32.
items.example: z = [1000, 999, 998]: naive exp overflows; z - 1000 gives stable π ≈ [0.67, 0.25, 0.09].

items.fix: Use sigmoid on each output independently with binary cross-entropy per label.
items.why: Softmax forces probabilities to sum to 1, incorrectly coupling independent labels.
items.example: Tags cat AND outdoor: two sigmoid heads with BCE, not one softmax over {cat, outdoor, neither}.

items.fix: Apply softmax (or use argmax on logits directly for prediction) before interpreting outputs as π_k.
items.why: Logits are unbounded and can be negative; only softmax outputs lie in (0,1) and sum to 1.
